objective: Create a ‘Fun Rainbow’ project using the code blocks.

concept_definitions.definition: Events blocks are like magical triggers that make your program respond to things happening around you! You can make things happen when you click a button, press a key, or even touch a sprite. It's all about creating interactive programs that come to life! For example, you can use the onEvent block to tell your program what to do when a specific event happens. It's like giving a secret code to your computer or device, and it knows exactly what to do when it sees that code. Remember, with events b

The setTimeout function is like a timer that allows us to delay the execution of a code block. It doesn't stop other code from running, but it schedules a specific function to be called after a certain time has passed. For example, think of it like a bank login that gets disabled if you take too long to enter your password. The setTimeout function helps us achieve such timing effects in our programs.

warmup_qa.q: How do we go to another screen in our project?
warmup_qa.a: We use a special code block called "setScreen".

warmup_qa.q: How did we show that an answer was wrong?
warmup_qa.a: We made the button's background turn red!

warmup_qa.q: What was your favorite part of creating our project?
warmup_qa.a:
Select any color for the screen from the properties section.
What is a SetImeout function?[2 marks]Answer: The setTimeout function is used to delay the execution of a piece of code.
 In which format time is displayed in the settimeout function? [2 marks]
Answer: Milliseconds.
What is the RGB color code for Red color ? [2 marks]
Answer: RGB(255,0,0).
(This is an assessment to recall the concepts learnt so far. Follow the checkpoint after this).

common_errors: Wrong RGB values for colors — remember RGB(255,0,0) is red
Using seconds instead of milliseconds (e.g. writing 5 instead of 5000)
Forgetting the callback function argument in setTimeout
